JAMES DEMARTINI, ESQ., 707.894.5000, FOR DEFENDNAT LEO J. LAROCCA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT LEO LAROCCA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS denied. A moving party's unilateral, unexpressed hope that another sibling will take care of the legal obligations arising from the lawsuit is not excusable neglect, and, in any event, failure to act on the default for several months is not appropriate diligence, and participation in a mediation is not a substitute for a responsive pleading.
